The easy idea is to follow the chapter's own clues one by one instead of memorising a loose definition. First notice the words and examples the text repeats: SOUND SOUND, SCIENCE 124, 10.1 Sound is Produced by a, SOUND 125. Then connect each example to the question the chapter is trying to answer. Like solving a source-backed puzzle, every useful point must come from the chapter text itself and stay traceable to the NCERT source.
full_notes:
Reaching the Age of Adolescence is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins with its own framing, examples and questions, then builds the topic through source-backed facts. The notes below keep that order: first the opening problem, then the important terms, then the mechanisms, examples and exam-useful connections that appear in the chapter itself.

The text explains that sOUND SOUND How do you come to know that a ‘period’ is over in your school?

The text explains that you come to know easily that someone is at your door when he knocks or you hear the sound of the doorbell.

The text explains that most of the time you can make out that someone is approaching you by just hearing the foot steps.

The text explains that you might have played a game called hide and seek.

The text explains that in this game a person is blind-folded and has to catch the remaining players.

The text explains that how is the blind- folded person able to guess which player is closest to her?

The text explains that we hear a variety of sounds in our surroundings.

The text explains that make a list of sounds you hear in your surroundings.

The text explains that in the music room of your school you hear the sounds produced by musical instruments like flute, tabla, harmonium, etc.

The text explains that how does it travel from one place to another ?

The text explains that we shall discuss such questions in this chapter.

The text explains that 10.1 : Some musical instruments Tabla Harmonium Sitar Flute 2024-25

The text explains that sCIENCE 124 10.1 Sound is Produced by a Vibrating Body Touch the school bell when not in use.

The text explains that hang it at a convenient place in such a way that it does not touch any wall.

The text explains that touch the plate or pan gently with your finger.

The text explains that 10.2 : Striking a pan Again strike the plate with the stick and hold it tightly with your hands immediately after striking.

The text explains that you can also make it with the help of an earthen pot (Fig.

The text explains that 10.4 : Vibrating dish produces waves in water Fig.

The text explains that this is the reason why the voices of men, women and children are different.

The text explains that 10.14 : A toy telephone We have learnt so far that vibrating objects produce sound and it is carried in all directions in a medium.

The text explains that the following table gives some idea of the loudness of sound coming from various sources.

The text explains that can you list some sources of noise pollution?

The text explains that because speech develops as the direct result of hearing, a child with a hearing loss may have defective speech also.

The text explains that society can do much to improve the living environment for the hearing-impaired and help them live normal lives.

The text explains that lack of sleep, hypertension (high blood- pressure), anxiety and many more health disorders may be caused by noise pollution.

The text explains that measures to Limit Noise Pollution To control noise, we must control the sources of noise.

The text explains that this process is called hearing. ‹ The number of oscillations or vibrations per second is called the frequency of oscillation. ‹ The frequency is expressed in hertz (Hz) ‹ Larger the amplitude of vibration, the louder is the sound. ‹ Higher the frequency of vibration, the higher is the pitch, and shriller is the sound. ‹ Unpleasant sounds are called noise. ‹ Excessive or unwanted sounds lead to noise pollution.

The text explains that list sources of noise pollution in your surroundings.

The text explains that explain in what way noise pollution is harmful to human.

The text explains that sketch larynx and explain its function in your own words.

The text explains that identify the sources of noise pollution in your locality.

The text explains that one of the marvels is the water supply system.

The text explains that discuss with your parents, friends and neighbours.

The text explains that prepare a brief report and present it in the class.

The text explains that you can read more on the related topics on the following websites: l www.physicsclassroom.com/Class/sound/soundtoc.html l health.howstuffworks.com/hearing.htm Golconda fort Did You Know?

The text explains that golconda fort, near Hyderabad, is one of the most magnificient forts in India.

The text explains that it is famous for many engineering and architectural marvels.

The text explains that but, perhaps, more astonishing is a dome near the entrance to the fort.

The text explains that a hand-clap at a particular point under the dome reverberates and can be heard at the highest point of the fort, about a kilometre away.

The text explains that if a guard saw a suspicious movement outside the fort, he clapped at the particular point under the dome, and the army inside the fort was alerted to the danger of the approaching enemy.

Source: NCERT Class 8 Science, Chapter 10: Reaching the Age of Adolescence
